steps.desc: Every element on a page is a box. Let's make one so we can see its parts.
Start with the content area itself. Width and height set how big the inside is.
Padding is the space inside the box, between the content and the edge.
The border wraps around the padding. You set its width, style, and color.
Margin is the space outside the border that pushes other boxes away.
By default, padding and border make a box bigger than its width. box-sizing: border-box keeps the width you asked for.
